narrative: OMX30 opens with tailwinds from a strong European session led by DAX outperformance (+1.15%) and broad positive momentum across Asia (Nikkei +3.14%). Technicals remain constructive: the index sits +9.3% above its 200-day MA, MACD is above signal with robust histogram (+4.272), and ADX 31.7 confirms a strong bullish trend. However, Stochastic K at 81.9 signals overbought conditions, requiring caution on mean reversion risk into any intraday weakness. Copper's +1.85% jump is a material tailwind for industrials and mining. USD/SEK's +0.28% move supports large-cap exporters. The main risk is profit-taking given we sit at 90% of 52-week range.
morningBrief: OMX30 expected to gap +0.3–0.5% higher on European strength and positive Asia spillover. Watch 3123 (yesterday close) as near-term support; resistance at 3180 (50-MA) and 3210–3230 (prior swing highs). Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo B, Sandvik) should lead on USD strength and copper upside; Boliden a core long on copper +1.85% and gold stability. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) trade sideways as US 10Y yields retreated -0.22%—watch for NIM support if yields stabilize. Ericsson and Hexagon steady on NASDAQ +0.64% but remain sentiment-dependent. Key intraday trigger: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET; any surprise could shift risk tone. Be mindful of Stochastic overbought signal (K=81.9)—profit-taking possible into rallies. Trade the industrial/commodity strength in the first hour; reassess after US data.
drivers: Copper surge +1.85% supports industrials and Boliden
Bullish MACD histogram (+4.272) and ADX 31.7 (strong trend)
OMX30 +9.3% above 200-MA in established bull market

sectorImpacts.reason: USD/SEK +0.28% supports export valuations; copper +1.85% reduces input cost headwinds; ADX 31.7 confirms bullish trend.
US 10Y yield fell -0.22% to 4.576%, compressing NIM expansion expectations. Wait for stabilization above 4.50% for positive NIM bias.
Copper +1.85% is a direct revenue driver for Boliden; gold stable. SSAB benefits indirectly from industrial momentum and USD strength.
NASDAQ +0.64% is modest support, but VIX decline to 16.59 removes risk-off defensive premium. Sentiment-dependent; no major catalyst.
Risk-on environment with VIX -1.01% reduces defensive rotation appeal. No healthcare-specific catalysts today.
US 10Y yields down -0.22%; refinancing costs remain elevated. Sector headwind until yields stabilize above 4.60%.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.85% is a strong direct revenue catalyst; gold holding firm. Outperformance likely if commodity momentum sustains.
USD/SEK +0.28% supports export margins; ADX 31.7 confirms trend. Industrial sector leader.
Dual exposure: USD strength (export pricing) and industrial momentum. Watch for 50-MA breakout.
NASDAQ +0.64% is mild support, but no major catalyst. Risk-on environment slightly unfavorable. Monitor tech sentiment.
Yields lower -0.22%; NIM expansion paused. Wait for 10Y above 4.60% for meaningful upside.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic data today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (forecast 219K) — potential swing catalyst. Trivial UK/EU releases (land tax commentary not data-driven).
topRisk: Stochastic K=81.9 (overbought) signals mean reversion risk into profit-taking; any disappointment on US Jobless Claims could trigger a VIX pop and equity pullback. Trade defensively into US data. Secondary risk: yuan weakness or China re-growth concerns could pressure copper from current levels.
technicalSummary: MACD above signal with histogram +4.272 (strong bullish divergence)
ADX 31.7 (strong trend, bullish DI bias) — high confidence in direction
RSI 60.82 neutral; no extreme overbought signal yet
Stochastic K=81.9, D=67.5 (overbought) — flag for mean reversion risk
